original_abstract:
Imbalanced learning represents one of the most enduring and formidable challenges in data mining and machine learning. Despite decades of continuous research, the ability to learn effectively from data characterized by skewed class distributions remains a critical bottleneck, often limiting the practical utility of both traditional machine learning and modern deep learning models in real-world scenarios. Recent breakthroughs have significantly deepened our conceptual understanding of these issues while simultaneously uncovering complex new hurdles. 

Given the rapid evolution of the field, this paper provides a comprehensive review of state-of-the-art strategies designed to mitigate class imbalance. Moving beyond typical surveys that focus almost exclusively on classification, we expand our scope to include emerging techniques in imbalanced regression and the nuances of deep long-tail learning. Furthermore, we map the landscape of practical applications, spanning a diverse range of disciplines from management science to industrial engineering. Finally, we identify and discuss nascent challenges and future research directions essential for advancing the frontier of imbalanced learning.
humanized_abstract:
One of the most persistent and toughest challenges in data mining and machine learning is the challenge of learning from imbalanced data. Despite five and half decades of research, learning from heavily skewed class distributions still remains one of the biggest bottlenecks to successful data mining and machine learning across a wide range of applications from classical machine learning to more modern deep learning approaches. The challenge has been studied in ever greater depth in recent years and it appears that it also has a number of new and not yet fully understood facets.

In view of the development momentum of the imbalance learning research field, this study conducts a survey for advanced methods designed to address class imbalance problem. This survey has moved beyond the scope of the ordinary survey focusing solely on classification due to the development of novel approaches for imbalanced regression, and deep long-tail learning, and even expands the applicable fields including management science and industrial engineering. In addition to conducting this survey of practical applications in various fields, we discuss future challenges and research works to be solved, to reinforce the development momentum of imbalance learning research field.

Imbalanced learning represents one of the most enduring and formidable challenges in data mining and machine learning.
